items: Myth busted: the Declaration of Independence and the Constitution are different documents with different purposes.
Myth busted: most delegates did not sign the Declaration on July 4; most signed the engrossed copy on August 2.
Myth busted: legal tender status does not require every private business to accept cash in every transaction.
Myth busted: “proof” describes a special minting process, not a coin’s condition grade.
Myth busted: Navajo Code Talkers did not simply speak ordinary Navajo; they used a specialized coded vocabulary.
Myth busted: basket “reed” made from rattan does not come from reed grass; rattan is a climbing palm.
Myth busted: tempera is not just watercolor with brighter pigment; it uses a different binder and behaves differently.
Myth busted: a proscenium is not the whole stage; it frames the opening between audience and playing space.
Myth busted: a composite is not one raw material; it combines reinforcing fibers with a surrounding matrix.
Myth busted: wood species alone does not determine performance; moisture, grain, grade, and dimensions also matter.
Myth busted: true, magnetic, and compass headings are not identical; wind, variation, and deviation all matter.
Myth busted: an end-of-train device is not merely a flashing light; it also monitors brake-pipe conditions.
Myth busted: boat ventilation is not just for comfort; it helps prevent explosive fuel-vapor buildup.
Myth busted: bright clothing improves bicycle visibility, but it does not replace a helmet, lights, or traffic rules.
Myth busted: trucks are not only last-mile delivery vehicles; they connect the entire national freight network.
Myth busted: UTM is not another name for latitude and longitude; it is a zone-based, meter-grid coordinate system.
Myth busted: a geocaching trackable is not a souvenir; log it and move it according to its stated mission.
Myth busted: HELP is not a swimming stroke; it is a heat-conserving posture used while awaiting cold-water rescue.
Myth busted: a heavy-duty trash bag is more than garbage storage—it can provide emergency rain protection or shelter.
Myth busted: a pioneering spar is not just any stick; it is a strong structural pole selected for lashing projects.
